title: Phase 7: keepalive discovery and systematic recovery map
notes: Phase 7 closed with a strong mapping exit. Final ledger: 148 substantive entries, 72 fruitful / 76 exploration. Direct keepalive discovery still not robust. Medium-source forceoracle->memoryreadout is now a verified 5-seed recovery branch. Strong-source partial-init transfer cleanly separates route transfer from content recovery. Keepalive-anchor adapter ES remains the strongest verified ES result, while medium and teacher sources show a consistent adapter-vs-router-only tradeoff. Phase-8 follow-up filed for teacher-seeded direct keepalive basin entry.
close_reason: Phase-7 strong mapping exit complete; report, lessons, scorecards, ledger, verification, and follow-up issue filed.

title: Phase 3: explicit keyed packet memory for delayed retrieval
description: Design and test a content-memory intervention that stores trigger payloads explicitly rather than relying on the current delay-carry path. Prioritize keyed or multi-slot memory plus a retrieval-aware auxiliary objective.
notes: Release-stage control finished. Starting from the same oraclewarm payloadaux checkpoint, masked release collapses back to phase-2 behavior (test 0.4473, delay_rate 0.0039, early_exit_rate 0.9922), while explicit no-mask release keeps real delayed routing (test 0.6328, delay_rate 0.7027, route_match 0.5130). This strongly implicates the metadata-driven train-time masks as the main cause of the old Benchmark B v2 policy collapse.
close_reason: Implemented and validated explicit packet memory plus release-stage experiments. The intervention changed Benchmark B v2 from immediate-exit collapse to a reproducible hard-routing improvement when the train-time masks are removed at release, and the remaining bottleneck is now tracked separately.

title: Implement packet-routing benchmarks and core model
description: Build the synthetic benchmarks, packet-routing temporal graph model, routing semantics, and shared utilities.
notes: Completed packet-routing core model and both synthetic benchmarks. Added hard/soft routing modes, mailbox DELAY semantics, oracle-routing benchmark, and long-horizon memory benchmark with reproducible seeded sampling.
close_reason: Implemented the packet-routing model, synthetic benchmarks, routing semantics, and supporting benchmark utilities.

title: Phase 2 audit Benchmark B and diagnose collapse
description: Audit Benchmark B v1, add oracle/heuristic analyses, and determine whether failure comes from benchmark design, objective incentives, or model/search limits.
notes: Audit complete enough to guide the second wave: Benchmark B v1 is a true delayed-memory task with a single oracle route and break-even CE that still favors delaying, but even near-perfect delayed routing stays at chance. Benchmark B v2 was added because v1 does not test adaptive routing; v2 makes delay objectively plausible and exposes validation collapse into easy-exit rather than successful delayed retrieval.
close_reason: Benchmark B audit completed. v1 was preserved as a fixed-route memory stress test, v2 was added as the adaptive-routing benchmark, and the collapse was localized to memory/generalization rather than reward impossibility.

title: Phase 6: controller discovery and staged recovery map
notes: Phase-6 update: the last strong-basin isolation reruns finished and both matched exactly. keepalive->memoryreadout_nosink reproduced at overall 0.9596 / route 0.9258 / fq 0.9793 / fq route 0.9706 / fq exit 125.92. keepalive->memoryonly reproduced at overall 0.9613 / route 0.9323 / fq 0.9826 / fq route 0.9739 / fq exit 126.13. With verification on the p1 runs, the reopening threshold is now tight: in the strong keepalive basin, reopened memory alone is already sufficient for near-perfect long-horizon recovery; readout and sink_proj are optional refinements; packet_adapter is not required. This closes the current recovery-ablation slice at a clean stopping point.

title: Phase 2 implement Benchmark B interventions and reruns
description: Implement at least three intervention families for Benchmark B collapse, run dev/main sweeps, and promote the best candidate to final seed comparisons.
notes: The completed dev hybrid write-aux pop64 run does not rescue delayed retrieval: train generations reached delay_rate around 0.7, delay_write_mean around 0.2-0.3, and route_match 1.0, but final test still collapsed to early_exit_rate 1.0 with delay_rate 0.0. Promoted T=256 hybrid main rerun on the stable mask-curriculum path shows the same collapse by the first validation checkpoint.
close_reason: Completed architecture, curriculum, and hybrid-ES intervention families; ran dev sweeps, promoted h256 reruns, and final 3-seed comparison. Conclusion is a strong negative on delayed-memory generalization with clear next bottleneck.

title: Phase 8: teacher-seeded direct keepalive basin entry
description: Use the verified route-faithful adapter-ES checkpoint as a fixed teacher for a direct keepalive student, distilling only wait/release/control-state channels before releasing into task-only hard routing. Goal: turn the phase-7 route-faithful teacher regime into a robust from-scratch discovery basin.
notes: Phase 8 closed as a strong mapping result. Strong keepalive-teacher wait/release-only supervision plus long release and delayed dropout created a real teacher-free route-faithful basin-entry effect across multiple seeds, but five-seed content quality stayed weak. The sharpest new boundary is that reopening memory destabilizes fragile teacher-free basins, while head-only reopenings preserve route but do not solve content. Follow-up filed as gnn2-vi6.
close_reason: Phase 8 campaign completed locally with docs, ledger, verification, and next-step issue filed as gnn2-vi6.

title: Phase 2 reporting, lessons, and handoff
description: Write phase2_plan, phase2_report, phase2_lessons, update README commands, and land/push all results with a clear next-step recommendation.
notes: README phase-2 commands and docs/phase2_lessons.md are in place. Final report regeneration and handoff will follow after the promoted h256 run and the seed sweep complete.
close_reason: Phase 2 plan, report, lessons, README commands, follow-up issues, and final handoff are complete. Remaining work is tracked in new phase-3 issues.

title: Run comparisons and write experiment report
description: Run smoke tests and at least one meaningful comparison suite, then produce the final markdown report with honest conclusions.
notes: Completed a dev comparison suite with Benchmark A method comparisons and ES ablations, plus Benchmark B horizon scaling runs and a generated markdown report with plots/tables.
close_reason: Ran the dev comparison suite and ablations, generated plots/tables, and wrote the final markdown experiment report.

title: Phase 5: multi-seed ES polish from working final-query checkpoints
description: Phase 4 found that scratch hybrid ES still collapses during warmstart on the improved architecture, but resume-based hybrid ES from the best phase4 hard-ST checkpoint reached perfect Benchmark B v2 performance on the same benchmark configuration. Follow up with a proper multi-seed panel that resumes from working hard-ST checkpoints, compares ES polish against additional gradient-only continuation, and measures whether ES local search is a reliable refinement stage even if it is not a robust discovery mechanism from scratch.
notes: Weak-basin role-map update: adapter+ES from seed947 achieves perfect route timing but only partial task recovery (confirm overall 0.9131, final-query acc 0.8276). Router-only ES from the same weak checkpoint is worse (confirm overall 0.8320, final-query acc 0.6668) while keeping route_match 1.0. Current interpretation: medium-basin rescue is controller-only, weak-basin rescue is limited by content quality and benefits from adapters.

title: Phase 3b: long-distance final-query retrieval after no-mask release
description: Target the remaining Benchmark B v2 bottleneck after the successful no-mask release result. The model now sustains real DELAY behavior and largely solves delay_to_trigger_exit, but delay_to_final_query remains near chance even with heavy delay usage and explicit packet memory. Focus next on longer-range retention/retrieval and query-conditioned decoding rather than on route willingness alone.
notes: Phase 4 complete. Scratch hybrid ES on the improved architecture still collapsed during warmstart (final-query acc ~0.24-0.25, route_match 0, exit_time 0), but timeout fix landed and a resume-based hybrid ES retest from the best sticky_aux_router2 hard-ST checkpoint was successful. Direct reevaluation of the resumed hard-ST checkpoint under the current eval loop gave overall 0.801 / final-query 0.593 / exit_time 77.34 / route_match 0.448. Resume-based hybrid ES on the same Benchmark B v2 configuration improved that single-seed checkpoint to overall 1.000 / final-query 1.000 / exit_time 127 / route_match 1.000 on 2 GPUs. Primary conclusion remains a strong negative on robust controller discovery across seeds; secondary conclusion is that ES polish looks promising once a working controller checkpoint already exists.

title: Add tests, configs, scripts, and reporting pipeline
description: Add reproducible configs, smoke/main scripts, unit tests, profiling, plotting, and report generation.
notes: Resumed after compaction. Confirmed missing deliverables are tests, run scripts, README expansion, and report generation. Current code and configs exist; next step is infrastructure completion plus any training/eval fixes needed for reporting.
close_reason: Added tests, configs, run scripts, report generation, README updates, and reproducible smoke/dev execution paths.

title: Phase 2 add routing and ES diagnostics
description: Add route-action, mailbox, TTL, confidence, reward-variance, and failure-slice diagnostics plus report plots for Benchmark B.
notes: Added phase-2 routing diagnostics, benchmark audit CLI, slice/per-mode metrics, mailbox/TTL/confidence stats, ES reward variance logging, and a phase2 report generator with plots under docs/phase2_assets/.
close_reason: Phase 2 diagnostics landed: route-action, transition, mailbox, TTL, confidence, slice, per-mode, and ES reward diagnostics plus updated report plots.

title: Bootstrap repository and Python environment
description: Initialize the gnn2 repository with uv, Python 3.12, PyTorch nightly cu130, task tracking, and agent preference notes.
notes: Resolved PyTorch nightly installation by pinning torch/torchvision/torchaudio to the CUDA 13.0 nightly index and triton to the base nightly index. Verified torch 2.12.0.dev20260317+cu130 with CUDA 13.0.
close_reason: Bootstrapped the repo with uv, Python 3.12, PyTorch nightly cu130, lockfile, task tracking, and persistent AGENTS preferences.

title: Import EGGROLL paper and reference code
description: Download the original EGGROLL paper, convert it to markdown, clone the HyperscaleES reference repo, compare the two, and record mandatory reference instructions in AGENTS.md.
notes: Downloaded the original EGGROLL paper and cloned HyperscaleES. Replaced the raw markdown conversion with a cleaner text-layer extraction in references/eggroll_paper.md, kept the old converter output as references/eggroll_paper_pymupdf4llm_raw.md, wrote docs/eggroll_reference_alignment.md, and updated AGENTS.md with mandatory EGGROLL reference discipline for later PyTorch work.
close_reason: Original EGGROLL paper and reference repo imported, cleaned, compared, and encoded into repo instructions.

title: Phase 5: stabilize final-query wait controller across seeds
description: Follow up on phase 4 strong-negative result. Phase 4 showed that explicit sticky control can produce real single-seed improvements on Benchmark B v2 delay_to_final_query, but promoted seed panels collapsed back to premature exit with zero route match. The next campaign should target controller robustness rather than basic memory capacity: factorized wait/not-wait controller, stronger temporal consistency on the wait latch, per-mode balancing, or direct route-head restructuring so the decodable control state reliably governs the hard router across seeds.
notes: Continuation update: hard-ST direct release-gate variants still failed to discover durable final-query behavior. Full-benchmark releasegate seeds 940/941 only pushed final-query exit to ~24.45 / ~33.87 with fq acc ~0.2265 / ~0.2570 and near-zero route_match. The finalquery-only direct-gate control run (seed952) was retired at step 99 after val stayed at chance (~0.2496) with fq route_match 0 and fq exit ~19.49. Current interpretation: hard-ST discovery remains the open problem; current ES results are stronger than the hard-ST fixes.

title: Phase 5 follow-up: content refinement after ES route rescue
description: Use the weak-basin phase-5 result as the starting point for a narrower follow-up. Resume from a weak hard-ST checkpoint such as results/phase5_dev/hard_st_b_v2_control_router2_setclear_oraclecontrol_opt_seed947_p1, use ES to recover perfect final-query routing, then freeze routing and run a short gradient-only content/readout refinement phase. The target is to lift final-query accuracy above the current ~0.83 weak-basin ES plateau without losing the recovered route timing.

title: Implement training methods and distributed low-rank ES
description: Add soft-routing, hard straight-through, and hybrid EGGROLL-inspired low-rank ES training with 2-GPU population evaluation.
notes: Implemented supervised soft and hard-ST training plus distributed 2-GPU low-rank hybrid ES with antithetic seed-reconstructed perturbations, warm-start support, checkpointing, and profiling outputs.
close_reason: Implemented soft and hard-ST baselines plus 2-GPU hybrid low-rank ES training with warm-start, checkpointing, and profiling.

title: Download arXiv paper 2603.12228 and convert to markdown
description: After repo setup is complete, download https://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.12228, convert it to markdown, and manually clean up the markdown output.
notes: Downloaded arXiv PDF 2603.12228, converted it to markdown, removed pagination and OCR debris, and manually repaired the title block, small tables, and obvious text artifacts.
close_reason: Downloaded arXiv paper 2603.12228, converted it to markdown, and cleaned the markdown by hand.

title: Investigate rank-1 instability in long warmstart 2-GPU hybrid h256 run
description: Reproduce and diagnose the late warmstart rank-1 failure seen in the original configs/phase2/main/hybrid_es_benchmark_b_v2_maskcurr_h256.yaml run. Determine whether the issue is distributed runtime, PyTorch nightly instability, or a model-side bug.
notes: The long h256 hybrid main attempt crashed on rank 1 after repeated collapsed validation checkpoints; a shorter stability-adjusted rerun completed and confirmed the same scientific conclusion.

title: Phase 10: route-regularized read-path adapter on decodable 1874
description: Start from the best confirmed phase-9 strong-source frozen-head baseline on teacher-free source 1874 and test a single tightly constrained read-path adapter with explicit route-drift regularization. Goal: improve held-confirm content without reopening broad memory or destabilizing late-route fidelity. Use the phase-9 locked-confirm suite as the primary guardrail and compare directly against the frozen query-gated finalqweight family and the query-FILM family.
notes: Phase 10 scaffold landed locally: docs/phase10_plan.md, docs/phase10_run_matrix.csv, scripts/run_phase10_*, src/utils/phase10_verify.py, and initial multiview/read-path adapter support in packet_routing.py. Full test gate passes (47 passed). Anchor reproductions launched: 1874 querygated finalqweight on GPU0 and 1874 queryfilm on GPU1.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: Phase 10 anchors updated locally: 9142 and 9132 reproduced with phase10_verify confirms in the expected regime; 9111 and 9102 confirm jobs are running on CPU. First real scouts are now live on both GPUs in dedicated run dirs after fixing a shared-results-dir launch mistake: strong-source multiview sink+packet+baseline query-gated fusion (seed10012) on GPU0 and strong-source low-rank read-path adapter (seed10022) on GPU1. Ledger updated in docs/phase10_run_matrix.csv.

title: Configure git remote and push experiment commits
notes: User provided remote URL git@github.com:catid/gnn2.git; configuring remote and pushing local commits.
close_reason: Configured origin as git@github.com:catid/gnn2.git and pushed master with the experiment suite, references, report, and verification artifacts.

title: Phase 9: head-only content shaping after teacher-seeded basin entry
description: Start from the verified phase-8 strong-teacher wait/release-only basin-entry recipe. Freeze the memory/router/control path once the student reaches a teacher-free route-faithful basin, then compare readout-only, sink+readout, and explicit content-focused auxiliary shaping. Goal: recover content without reopening memory and destabilizing the basin.
notes: Phase 9 closeout: Cluster A settled the core split cleanly. Strong direct-entry 1874, medium 1842, and upper-bound 1201 are strongly decodable frozen sources; fragile 1879 is weak-content despite route fidelity. Strict head-only shaping on 1879 is now a fair five-seed negative, while strong-source query-gated finalqweight is the strongest aggregate confirmed head-only family and query-FILM is the strongest alternative-reader family. Medium-source readout-only 9111 also closed as a stable head-only family but remains confirmation-limited. Minimal-safe read-path touches and head-only ES did not beat the best gradient readers. Recommended next step is a single route-regularized read-path adapter on the strongly decodable 1874 source, starting from the best frozen-reader baseline rather than more fragile-source work.
close_reason: Phase 9 complete: strong mapping exit delivered with frozen-state audit, seven full five-seed panels, confirmed strong-source head-only family, confirmed medium-source head-only family, fair fragile-family negative, and gated minimal-safe / head-only ES boundaries.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: Phase 9 pivot tightened: fragile 1879 head-only family remains a fair negative after 9101/9102/9104/9108, with exact rerun 9102 queued. Strong-source alternative-reader branch is the new lead: 9130 verify reached base 0.8115 overall / 0.6334 fq_acc / 0.9482 fq_route / 122.04 exit, and 9142 verify reached 0.8838 / 0.7695 / 0.9385 / 121.31, but both remain confirm-fragile at full_locked ~0.32-0.33 fq_acc with route ~0.877. Launched 9142 rerun + first panel seeds, and launched 9150 query-gated + content-distill to test whether confirm collapse is objective-related rather than missing frozen content.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: Phase 9 mid-campaign update: strong-source query-gated finalqweight family now has a full five-seed panel across 9142/9146/9147/9148/9149. Current means: base 0.8488 overall / 0.7004 fq_acc / 0.9416 fq_route / 121.49 fq_exit; full_locked 0.6534 overall / 0.3237 fq_acc / 0.8771 fq_route / 115.49 fq_exit. So the family is robustly better on base content than older heads, but still confirmation-limited. Fragile-family rerun 9102_rerun1 independently verifies the expected negative boundary: base 0.2488 / 0.2513 / 0.8910 / 122.95, full_locked 0.2498 / 0.2396 / 0.9909 / 127.00, finalquery-heavy 0.2405 / 0.2462 / 0.9845 / 126.98. That confirms the phase-8 story that fragile 1879 retains route but not recoverable content under strict head-only shaping.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: Phase 9 update: Cluster A decodability split is now stable (1874/1842/1201 strongly decodable; 1879 weakly decodable only). Cluster B fragile head-only family is now a fair multi-seed negative: 9102_rerun1 plus 9311-9314 preserve route/exit under locked confirms but stay near-chance on fq_acc. Cluster D content-distill boundaries 9141/9143/9144 are verify-clean overfit cases: strong base content but collapse to ~0.17-0.20 overall on full_locked while keeping route. Cluster E tiny read-path touches 9301-9303 preserve route but do not give a meaningful content lift, so 9341-9344 are only being run as the final fair panel for the best tiny-touch variant. Live promoted work is now the strong-source 9151-9154 query-gated content-distill panel on GPU0 and the tail of Cluster G head-only ES on GPU1; after that GPU1 will take the 9331-9334 query-FILM panel unless G produces a stronger rerun candidate.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: Phase-9 late-state: 9153 landed strong (test 0.8714 / 0.7360 / 0.9332 / 120.82), 9154 still running; fragile E panel has 9341 complete and 9342 landed in the same weak-content route-faithful regime. Important bookkeeping correction: cluster C medium-source head-only coverage is underrepresented in the matrix, so before closeout I am adding the already-run 9135/9136/9145 medium variants to the ledger and running the missing 9111-9114 medium-source head-only scouts plus reruns for 9116 and 9145. Also planning 9202_rerun1 for cluster G and 9301_rerun1 for cluster E so the exact-rerun floor and cluster-specific rerun requirements are satisfied honestly.
